answer:
Twilio APIs use HTTP Basic authentication [1][2]. For production applications, Twilio recommends using an API key as the username and its corresponding API key secret as the password [1][3]. For local testing, you may use your Account SID as the username and your Auth Token as the password [1][2]. You can generate API keys or retrieve your Account SID and Auth Token through the Twilio Console [1][4].

```bash
curl -G https://api.twilio.com/2010-04-01/Accounts \
  -u $YOUR_API_KEY:$YOUR_API_KEY_SECRET
```

results.title: REST API: Key Resource v2010 | Twilio
